name: Commercial Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Community bank serving Southeast Arkansas with multiple branches in Monticello, Warren, Hermitage, and Hampton, offering online banking, mobile apps, and traditional deposit accounts.
description_long:
Commercial Bank and Trust Company is a regional community bank headquartered in Monticello, Arkansas, with a service area spanning Southeast Arkansas. The bank operates under federal banking regulations and maintains compliance with Federal Reserve Bank and American National Bank holiday schedules. The institution has been serving its local communities for an extended period, establishing itself as a trusted financial services provider in rural Arkansas.

The bank offers individualized banking and account options tailored to customer needs, including online banking with 24/7 access to accounts and bill payment functionality. Customers can download mobile banking applications for both Apple and Android platforms to manage their finances remotely. Commercial Bank also provides traditional banking services through eight physical branch locations across four towns: five locations in Monticello (Main/Downtown, Bookkeeping, Westgate, East, and North Park), two in Warren (Main and Martin & Pine), and single locations in Hermitage and Hampton. All branches feature 24-hour ATM access.

Commercial Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused approach, emphasizing personalized service to "community people you know." The bank operates extended hours at select locations, with some drive-thrus open until 6 pm on Fridays and limited Saturday service in Monticello and Warren. The institution maintains a 24-hour bank line for customer inquiries and provides after-hours lost/stolen card reporting through a dedicated hotline. The bank's website includes comprehensive cyber security information and multiple privacy disclosures regarding internet security and third-party links.

As a regional community bank, Commercial Bank's service area is geographically limited to Southeast Arkansas, which restricts accessibility for customers outside this region. The bank does not appear to offer specialized products like investment services, wealth management, business lending with detailed product information, or advanced digital features comparable to larger regional or national banks. For customers seeking comprehensive financial services beyond basic deposit accounts and online banking, or those outside the Arkansas service area, this institution may have limitations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Bill payment functionality through online platform
Mobile banking applications for iOS and Android
Individualized deposit accounts tailored to customer needs
In-person banking at eight branch locations
Lost and stolen debit/ATM card reporting
Saturday drive-thru service at select Monticello locations
Extended Friday hours at multiple branches
pros: 24/7 online banking with bill pay access from anywhere
Mobile banking apps available on both Apple and Android platforms
Eight physical branch locations across four Southeast Arkansas towns with consistent accessibility
24-hour ATM access at all branch locations
Extended hours on Fridays with some locations open until 6 pm
24-hour bank line for customer service inquiries
After-hours lost/stolen card reporting available via dedicated hotline
Local community bank with personalized service approach
cons: Service area limited to Southeast Arkansas only—not accessible to customers in other regions
Limited product information on website; no details on interest rates, fees, or account types
No evidence of specialized services like business lending, investment management, or wealth advisory
Modest digital presence compared to regional or national banks with more advanced platforms
Saturday lobby hours unavailable except at Monticello Bookkeeping location; most locations closed weekends
best_for: Southeast Arkansas residents seeking local community banking with personal service
Customers who value traditional branch banking combined with online and mobile access
Small business owners in the Monticello, Warren, Hermitage, and Hampton areas
Rural customers preferring a local institution they can visit in person
diagnosis: Commercial Bank and Trust Company is best suited for customers in Southeast Arkansas seeking traditional community banking with both in-person and digital access. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank only serves Monticello, Warren, Hermitage, and Hampton, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
